understanding.goal_understanding: Progress through all KA59 levels by positioning every selectable piece onto the goal tiles marked '+' at the same time; if a level has multiple goals, each must be occupied simultaneously for WIN, and completing the final WIN ends the whole run.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: The currently selected piece (shown as 'P' in the semantic grid) moves one cell per MOVE action on a 3-pixels-per-cell coordinate system, with positions reported as pixel-aligned [x,y]. CLICK switches control to another selectable located at the specified pixel position (the same coordinate system used in the state/object lists). Blocks can be pushable: if a block is adjacent in the move direction and the space beyond allows, the move can push the block along. Walls/boundaries (#) can block movement, though the movement_*_blocked flags are only heuristic indicators of adjacency and do not guarantee the move will fail; actual behavior must be confirmed by attempting moves and observing interactions.
